text:
First (supervised) FSD approval in Europe!

Congratulations to the Tesla team and thank you to the regulatory authorities in the Netherlands for all the hard work required to make this happen. https://t.co/8hidEOPSxm
summary: First (supervised) FSD approval in Europe! Congratulations to the Tesla team and thank you to the regulatory authorities in the Netherlands for all the hard work required to make this happen.
ai_summary: Tesla achieved its first supervised Full Self-Driving (FSD) approval in Europe, specifically in the Netherlands, following regulatory authorization. This milestone demonstrates Tesla's autonomous driving technology advancement and the company's ability to navigate complex regulatory frameworks across different markets—a critical business challenge for scaling autonomous vehicle technology globally.
embed_html:
First (supervised) FSD approval in Europe!

Congratulations to the Tesla team and thank you to the regulatory authorities in the Netherlands for all the hard work required to make this happen. https://t.co/8hidEOPSxm

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 10, 2026

text:
Tesla is down to the last few hundred Model S & X cars in inventory. 

Poignant end of an era. https://t.co/2ZtIL8Nzmd
summary: Tesla is down to the last few hundred Model S & X cars in inventory. Poignant end of an era.
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that Tesla is down to the last few hundred Model S & X vehicles in inventory, signaling an end to production of these legacy vehicle lines. This reflects Tesla's strategic shift away from its original flagship models toward newer platforms and production priorities, demonstrating how mature product lines naturally phase out as companies evolve their manufacturing focus and product portfolio.
embed_html:
Tesla is down to the last few hundred Model S & X cars in inventory.

Poignant end of an era. https://t.co/2ZtIL8Nzmd

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 10, 2026

text: Tesla Semi feels like a sports car to drive https://t.co/an2KiEh5wG
summary: Tesla Semi feels like a sports car to drive
ai_summary: Elon Musk shares a firsthand observation about the Tesla Semi's driving dynamics, describing it as having sports car-like handling characteristics despite being a heavy commercial vehicle. This suggests Tesla's engineering approach to the Semi prioritizes driver experience and vehicle responsiveness alongside freight hauling capability, indicating product design decisions that balance performance with practical commercial trucking requirements.
embed_html:
Tesla Semi feels like a sports car to drive https://t.co/an2KiEh5wG

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 12, 2026
